$schema: Documents the MCP-sourced tool contracts callable by the browser agent. This is a REVIEWABLE ARTIFACT, not a file loaded at runtime — the live, authoritative tool list is always whatever MCPToolManager.get_tools() discovers (built-in browser-use bridge server in Backend/src/agents/browser_use_bridge.py, plus any server declared in Backend/src/agents/mcp_servers.json). If a tool is renamed/added/removed in the MCP server, update this file to match — it must never drift into being a second, hardcoded tool registry that code depends on.
sourcing_policy: MCP-only. No @tool/StructuredTool definitions exist outside this registry (enforced by removing the standalone Tavily tool in Task #154 Step 1).
tool_filter_mechanism: Backend/src/agents/spec_loader.py:filter_tools — glob pattern(s) in an agent spec's `tool_filter` frontmatter field matched against live tool names. Browser agent uses tool_filter: "*" (all discovered tools).
error_handling: Tool exceptions are caught in Backend/src/services/brain.py:_call_tool and turned into an `Error executing tool: <message>` observation string rather than propagating; this drives the SUCCESS/FAILED status and the Verifier's BLOCKING classification.

browser_navigate.description: Navigate the active page to a URL.
browser_navigate.validation: Verifier (src/services/verifier.py) checks the resulting ObservedState.url's host against the requested target host; a mismatch is surfaced as a WARNING (informational — may be a legitimate redirect), not a hard failure.
error_codes: browser closed/crashed mid-navigation -> BROWSER_CRASH recovery branch
browser_navigate.rate_limits: None enforced by the tool itself; site-side rate limiting surfaces as a TRANSIENT_BLOCK recovery branch.

browser_click.description: Click an element by index (from browser_get_state) or coordinates.
browser_click.validation: Verifier flags BLOCKING if the observation shows a CAPTCHA/OTP/error signal immediately after the click; flags INFO if the page is still loading (confirm effect next turn).
error_codes: element not found -> MISSING_ELEMENT recovery branch

browser_type.description: Type text into the currently focused (or specified) input element.
browser_type.validation: Same as browser_click. Secret values (passwords/OTP codes) are redacted before this call's args/output are persisted — see obstacle_handler.py:_redact_secret_values_in_text.
browser_type.permissions: Never called with credentials outside CONFIRMED INPUTS per browser.agent.md Safety Rules.

browser_get_state.description: Returns the current interactive-element summary and page text used to build ObservedState.
browser_get_state.validation: Feeds directly into src/services/observer.py:observe() — this is the primary source of ObservedState.raw_text.

browser_screenshot.description: Capture a screenshot of the current viewport.

browser_scroll.description: Scroll the page or a scrollable container.

browser_get_html.description: Return the raw HTML of the current page (sanitized before reaching the LLM — see _sanitize_browser_observation).

browser_evaluate.description: Execute custom JavaScript code on the page (for advanced interactions, shadow DOM, custom selectors, data extraction).

browser_close_session.description: Close the entire browser session; triggers session cleanup in brain.py._call_tool.

browser_search_google.description: Run a Google search and return results.

browser_key_press.description: Press a keyboard key or combination on the currently focused element.

browser_hover.description: Move the mouse to an element or pixel coordinates without clicking.

browser_wait.description: Pause execution for a specified number of milliseconds (used after typing/clicking to let autocomplete/validation settle).

browser_drag.description: Click-and-drag from one set of pixel coordinates to another.

extra_mcp_servers.description: Any server declared in Backend/src/agents/mcp_servers.json is merged into the live tool list automatically; its tools are unnamed here by design since the list is dynamic and site-specific. See Backend/src/agents/mcp_servers.example.json for the declaration format.
extra_mcp_servers.conflict_policy: A user-declared server cannot redefine a built-in server name; the built-in config takes precedence (Backend/src/agents/mcp_agent.py).
